title: galeopsis tetrahit subsp. pubescens
intro: Galeopsis tetrahit subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy gallweed, is a subspecies of the Galeopsis tetrahit plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to reduce pain and prevent infection. The health benefits of Galeopsis tetrahit subsp. pubescens include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual cramps. This plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
